definitions.text: A mark left by something that has passed along.
examples: Follow the track of the ship.
Can you see any tracks in the snow?

definitions.text: A mark or impression left by the foot, either of man or animal.
examples: The fox tracks were still visible in the snow.

definitions.text: The entire lower surface of the foot; said of birds, etc.
To continue over time. To observe the (measured) state of a person or object over time.
examples: We will track the raven population over the next six months.
Agent Miles has been tracking the terrorist since Madrid.

definitions.text: To observe the (measured) state of a person or object over time.
examples: We will track the raven population over the next six months.

definitions.text: To monitor the movement of a person or object.
examples: Agent Miles has been tracking the terrorist since Madrid.
